titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William Steele West Family Papers
titlestmt.author: Stephanie Bredbenner

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday January 12, 2024 at 04:42

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This material is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from McBride Rare Books on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2021.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. William Steele West Papers, 1803-1941. II. Margaret West Goodrich Family Papers, 1889-1987. III. Photographs and Portraits, circa 1839-1972. IV. Realia, circa 1890-1959.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William Steele West (1872-1941) was a lawyer, judge, and businessman. He was born in Austin, Texas to Charles Shannon West (1829-1885), a lawyer and judge, and Florence Randolph DuVal West (1840-1881). William Steele West studied at University of Texas and Cumberland University before working for a bank and later a utilities company. Circa 1900, West became manager of the Wild Goose Placer Mining & Trading Company and moved to Alaska. He married Josephine Johnson West in 1902 and their only child, Margaret West Goodrich, was born in 1904. The family settled in Brownsville, Texas circa 1910, where William Steele West began a law practice and later became a judge. William Steele West was also a member of the Republican State Executive Committee and a board member of several organizations in Texas.

Margaret West Goodrich (1904-1980) was the daughter of William Steele West and Josephine Johnson West. In 1928, she married Emmette Knepp Goodrich. The couple later moved to Mexico City, Mexico and had two children, Joe Anna Goodrich and William Newman Goodrich.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William Steele West Family Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection documents the life, career, and family of William Steele West, particularly his life in Alaska and work with the Wild Goose Placer Mining & Trading Company. Present are West's diaries and correspondence during his time in Alaska. Several photograph albums contain photographs of the mining operations of the Wild Goose Placer Mining & Trading Company, mining facilities, and miners, including Japanese miners. Many of the mining photographs were taken by A. B. Kinne (1853-1925). There are also photographs of Eskimos and other Alaska Natives, as well as informal family photographs and formal portraits. The collection also contains a small quantity of William Steele West's personal papers, professional papers relating to his later career, his daughter Margaret West Goodrich's correspondence, financial records, and legal papers, other personal and family papers, and realia, including commemorative spoons, ivory souvenirs, and beaded gloves from Alaska.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by William Steele West are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on the use of these materials. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in rough chronological order, with materials relating to Josephine Johnson West's family filed at the end.

did.unittitle: William Steele West Austin High School report card

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries in the diary document William Steele West's life in Alaska, including the winter of 1900-1901 in Ophir, Alaska. Later entries describe his time in Nome, Alaska in 1902, when his journey to inspect mining claims was disrupted by heavy ice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript copy of diary entries recounting William Steele West's journey from Nome, Alaska to San Francisco, California in 1901. The manuscirpt original is not present in the collection.

did.unittitle: Excerpts from The Land of Nome by Lanier McKee
Correspondence and report concerning Wild Goose Mining & Trading Company

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript copy of diary entries recounting William Steele West's journey from Seattle, Washington to Nome, Alaska in 1909. The manuscript original is not present in the collection.

did.unittitle: Resolution in memory of William Steele West
Anecdotes concerning mining and daily life in Alaska

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: These anecdotes may recount specific events in William Steele West's life, but they may also be excerpts from a work of fiction set in Alaska.

did.unittitle: Legal and financial documents concerning the Johnson family

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in rough chronological order, with undated materials and genealogical research materials filed at the end.

did.unittitle: Letters to Emmette Knepp Goodrich and Margaret West Goodrich
Margaret West Goodrich letters to William Steele West and Josephine Johnson West
School report by Joe Anna Goodrich
Joe Anna Goodrich Columbia University Vernon prize
Art collected by Margaret West Goodrich
Emmette Knepp Goodrich and Margaret West Goodrich financial and legal papers
Postcards to Joe Anna Goodrich Enriquez de Rivera
Margaret West Goodrich Bible and Goodrich Castle bookmark

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Photograph Albums, Cased Photographs and Framed Portraits, and Other Phhotographs and Portraits.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph album containing photographs depicting several locations in Alaska including Nome, Council, Candle, and Ophir. Many of the photographs document the operations of Wild Goose Mining & Trading Company, including mining facilities, a sawmill, freight shipments, and dogsleds. Also present are panoramas, photographs of Alaska landscapes, and family photographs. Many of the photographs concerning Wild Goose Mining & Trading Company were taken by A. B. Kinne.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Note that this album contains many loose pages and photographs. Handle with caution.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph album containing photographs depicting several locations in Alaska, including the Niukluk River, Nome, and Golovin Mission. Many of the photographs depict Eskimo communities, including fishing villages, women cleaning fish, a gravesite, and formal portraits. Some Eskimo individuals are identified by name in the captions. The subjects of other photographs include dogsledding, landscapes, and freight boats. Most of the photographs in this album were taken by A. B. Kinne.

did.unittitle: Photograph album of Alaska and Texas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph album containing photographs depicting Alaska and Texas. Subjects in the Alaska photographs include mining operations, landscapes, Eskimo people, dogs, and family photographs. The Texas photographs are primarily family photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph album containing family photographs taken in Texas. Subjects include formal and informal portraits, family vacations, fishing, automobiles, trains, and a women's suffrage club founded by young girls in Brownsville, Texas.

did.unittitle: Photograph album of Texas and Mexico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph album containing family photographs taken in Texas and Mexico. Subjects include fishing, hunting, and family vacations.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Note that this album contains many loose pages and photographs. Handle with caution.

did.unittitle: Ambrotype of an unidentified woman and child
Framed portrait of Margaret West Goodrich
